text: 2.3g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. fat is basically not the story here.
73.5g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for grains, the carb quality matters much more than the raw number.
12.5g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein helps, but not enough if the carb side is weak.
everything else here is pretty flat.
magnesium at 33% daily value, iron at 20% daily value, zinc at 19% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

on the plus side: useful staple energy source. that matters because grains are often judged by how practical they are in real diets. usually affordable and scalable. practicality matters for staple foods. works as a meal anchor. a lot of value comes from being easy to build around.

now the downsides: protein quality is limited. even the better grains usually need pairing. quality can drop fast with processing. the grain category punishes refinement pretty hard. easy to overshoot portions. staple carbs can pile up fast without much friction.
A tier. Best when you want a staple carb that actually brings something useful with it.
